subject: Re: "Unhandled Exception" when trying Noise2map on tomogram
body:
Hi Florian,

Thanks for the suggestion!

I still get the same error unfortunately. 

I think invoking the flag must an implicit 'true' then.

Thanks again,
-Tom

On Friday, February 19, 2021 at 10:29:58 PM UTC-8 Florian Fäßler wrote:

> Hi Tom,
>
> as far as I can tell, a boolean missing after --dont_flatten_spectrum:
>
> noise2map --observation1 even --observation2 odd --dont_flatten_spectrum 
> true --iterations 15000 --batchsize 1 --gpuid_network 0 
> --gpuid_preprocess 0
>
> I hope this helps.
>
> Cheers,
> Florian
> thomas.la...@gmail.com schrieb am Freitag, 19. Februar 2021 um 23:51:09 
> UTC+1:
>
>> Hi everybody!
>>
>> Running into a bit of a snag trying to denoise a tomogram using Noise2map.
>>
>> When I run:
>> noise2map --observation1 even --observation2 odd --dont_flatten_spectrum 
>> --iterations 15000 --batchsize 1 --gpuid_network 0 --gpuid_preprocess 0
>>
>> I get:
>> Unhandled Exception: System.IO.IOException: The handle is invalid
>>
>>     at System.IO._Error.WinIOError(Int32 errorCode, String maybeFullPath)
>>     at System.Console.GetBufferInfo(Boolean throwOnNoConsole, Boolean& 
>> succeeded)
>>     at Noise2Map.Program.ClearCurrentConsoleLine() in 
>> D:\Dev2\warp2\Noise2Map\Program.cs: line 614
>>     at Noise2Map.Program.Main(String[ ] args) in 
>> D:\warp2\Noise2Map\Program.cs:line 489
>>
>> Note sure what to do differently...
>>
>> Suggestions welcome.
>>
>> Cheers,
>> -Tom
>>
>
